project_overview.project_name: Santa Cruz Road Corridor Connector Project (San Ignacio – San Jose)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve transport accessibility along the road corridor between San Ignacio de Velasco and San Jose de Chiquitos
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 125,000 inhabitants in four municipalities (San José de Chiquitos, San Rafael, San Miguel, and San Ignacio de Velasco), of which 51% are poor and 62% are of indigenous descent; businesses and road users along the corridor

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: no - Bolivia is not classified as fragile or conflict-affected, though the project area has high poverty rates (47.7% to 74%) and limited accessibility

key_components.description: Upgrading approximately 208 km of primary road network (RF-017) from gravel to asphalt concrete pavement, including construction of three bypasses (San Ignacio de Velasco, San Miguel, San Rafael), three small bridges, approximately 300 culverts, and alignment improvements. Works supervision and technical audits included.

Financing feasibility studies (technical, economic, environmental, social) and bid document preparation for the San Matias-San Ignacio de Velasco road corridor (300 km) using DBMOT contracting approach; gender studies and PSIAs; project management, audits, training, and capacity building for road asset management

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Administradora Boliviana de Carreteras (ABC) - a decentralized entity of the Ministry of Public Works, Services and Housing (MOPSV) with technical, administrative, and financial autonomy
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: ABC's Construction Unit handles civil works planning and M&E; Environmental and Social Department (GSA) oversees environmental and social supervision; regional offices in Santa Cruz have responsibility for operational aspects; Social Oversight Committees (SOCs) composed of civil society organizations monitor implementation; Grievance Redress Committee linked to municipal legal services (SLIMs)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Works procured through single International Competitive Bidding (ICB) process; supervision contract through international selection (QCBS); DBMOT study consultant through QCBS; World Bank procurement guidelines and standard bidding documents used; prior review for contracts over thresholds
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: ABC's Construction Unit sub-director responsible for M&E; semiannual reports on financial and physical progress; Quality Management System (ISO 9001:2008) implemented; citizen engagement through beneficiary population monitoring; iRAP surveys for road safety; Midterm Review planned for November 2018

identified_risks.risk_description: ABC has shown weaknesses in contract management and procurement of large contracts; institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability is limited
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: RSCDP (P144597) provides comprehensive support for contract management capacity development through multiyear specialized firm engagement, direct staff support, training programs, and curriculum development; World Bank systematic training on procurement; close monitoring during first two years

identified_risks.risk_description: FM systems have shortcomings; complex implementation arrangements requiring regional office involvement; complex budgeting and accounting arrangements; inherent, control, and overall FM risks all rated Substantial
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ABC implementing integrated FM information system (SIGEP-SIAP); revised and streamlined processes and procedures with improved internal controls; new format and content for interim financial reports; confirmation from Ministry of Economy on SIGEP-SIAP timeline; Operational Manual with detailed procedures; annual audits by independent firm; FM supervision missions at least twice yearly

identified_risks.risk_description: ABC's technical and fiduciary teams have inadequate knowledge of World Bank procurement procedures; potential for no offers or market response; timely supply of materials for civil works
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Bid documents prepared according to World Bank standards; RSCDP support for contract management capacity; Operational Manual addressing procurement procedures; World Bank systematic training program; close monitoring particularly in first two years; semiannual implementation support missions

identified_risks.risk_description: Labor influx of approximately 2,000 workers over three years poses risks to vulnerable populations, particularly Ayoreo people; potential for gender-based violence, unwanted pregnancies, HIV/AIDS/STD transmission, acculturation, and illegal land occupation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive labor influx mitigation measures including: mandatory Code of Conduct for workers with annual training; four labor camps with site-specific environmental and social management plans; contractor and supervision consultant contractually bound to safeguard implementation; Grievance Redress Mechanism linked to SLIMs and Defender of Children; Social Oversight Committees; information campaigns on Code of Conduct and GBV prevention; IPP projects for economic empowerment and health promotion; monitoring of contractor compliance; ABC's well-staffed Environmental and Social Division

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential adverse environmental impacts including groundwater contamination from labor camps, dust affecting historical monuments, disturbance of fauna, waste disposal issues
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: EIA/EMP with detailed mitigation programs (PASAs and PPMs); labor camps located at least 2 km from population centers and 500 m from watercourses; specific design measures to prevent aquifer contamination; waste management and wastewater treatment at camps; site restoration and closure provisions; contractor's CESMP with site-specific assessments; supervision consultant environmental team oversight; quarterly compliance audits

identified_risks.risk_description: Macroeconomic pressures from declining commodity prices and fiscal deficits; potential for reduced public investment allocations to road sector
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project supports Government's PDES and strategic connectivity priorities; World Bank engagement in sector policy dialogue; complementary RSCDP support for institutional strengthening

identified_risks.risk_description: Final design for San Ignacio de Velasco bypass not yet completed; potential for design changes affecting resettlement
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: RPF prepared to address potential resettlement from bypass; site-specific RAP to be prepared after final design completion; feasibility and design studies for bypass to be financed under project; World Bank review and concurrence required

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Category B - potential adverse environmental impacts are site-specific, reversible, and can be readily mitigated
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-specific Grievance Redress Mechanism (Sistema de Atención de Consultas y Reclamos) established with Committee including ABC management, Environmental and Social Division representative, affected people representative, Contractor social specialist, and Supervision Consultant specialist; full-time ABC staff member assigned; electronic logging and tracking system; coordination with municipal legal services (SLIMs) and Defender of Children and Adolescents (DNA); referral protocols for gender-based violence cases under Law 348; World Bank Grievance Redress Service also available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender issues identified include: lack of sustainable economic opportunities for indigenous women; Ayoreo women involvement in commercial sex work and vulnerability to STDs/HIV-AIDS; single mothers and heads of households suffering gender violence; weak mechanisms to report gender violence. IPP incorporates gender focus in all activities including: six projects for economic empowerment of Chiquitano women; projects for Ayoreo women's economic alternatives to sex work; health projects on HIV/AIDS/STD prevention; GBV prevention training for construction workers; support to strengthen SLIMs and access to justice; workshops on gender-based violence prevention
vulnerable_groups: Indigenous Peoples (Chiquitano - 61% of project area population; Ayoreo - 0.066% but highly vulnerable)
Women (particularly indigenous women, single mothers, heads of households)
Ayoreo women engaged in commercial sex work

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project area is not in a conflict-affected zone. However, project design incorporates conflict-sensitive approaches: extensive consultation with indigenous communities (Chiquitano and Ayoreo) at national, regional, and community levels; IPP designed to strengthen indigenous identities and cultures to mitigate acculturation; territory management and land protection programs to prevent illegal occupation by new immigrants; Social Oversight Committees for community participation and monitoring; grievance mechanisms to address community concerns; gender-based violence prevention strategies; Code of Conduct for workers addressing ethnic and gender-based violence
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Project area characterized by high poverty (47.7% to 74% across municipalities vs. 39% national average), limited accessibility, weak infrastructure services, and geographic isolation. Key fragility factors: limited access to markets and services; high school dropout rates (18% of school-aged children not in school); limited electricity access (33-44% without grid connection); inadequate sanitation (less than 10% with piped sewage); informal sector employment dominance; geographic remoteness of rural populations. Project addresses fragility through improved connectivity, reduced transport costs, and enhanced access to employment, education, and health services
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Labor influx of 2,000 workers over three years poses security risks including potential for increased crime, gender-based violence, and illicit behavior. Mitigation measures include: mandatory Code of Conduct with sanctions; contractor monitoring of personnel entry/exit; coordination with local law enforcement; GBV prevention training; Grievance Redress Mechanism; Social Oversight Committees; information campaigns; police presence enhancement; contractor's social team community liaison role
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Involuntary resettlement triggered: 386 properties physically affected (339 individual, 47 communitarian); 36 families in La Fortuna community and 2 families in Quituquina community will be resettled within their communities; 60 houses, 199 fences, and various other structures require replacement or compensation; no land acquisition or expropriation needed; RAP prepared for known impacts; RPF prepared for potential San Ignacio bypass impacts; cash compensation financed by counterpart; accompaniment activities until livelihoods restored to pre-displacement levels or better; ABC responsible for RAP/RPF implementation
